seo.title: AI Agent Hallucination Detection in AutoGen Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent hallucination detection in AutoGen agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In AutoGen, AI agent hallucination manifests as agents generating factually incorrect information during multi-agent conversations, often leading to cascading errors across the conversation chain. This is particularly problematic in group chat scenarios where one agent's hallucinated output influences others, resulting in collective agreement on false conclusions. Hallucinations frequently appear in code generation tasks, factual queries, or when agents reference non-existent tools and APIs specific to AutoGen's agent patterns.

howItManifests.symptom: Agents cite non-existent AutoGen APIs like `agent.execute_code()` or invent tool names like `web_search_plus`
howItManifests.impact: All agents converge on incorrect implementation, wasting tokens and producing broken code

howItManifests.symptom: Assistant agent suggests `import vex_sdk` or `from autogen.tools import hallucination_detector`
howItManifests.impact: CodeExecutor fails with ModuleNotFoundError, halting conversation and requiring human intervention

howItManifests.symptom: Agents claim 'AutoGen v0.4.2 achieved 98% hallucination reduction per Microsoft benchmarks' (false)
howItManifests.impact: Decision-making agents select wrong strategies based on fabricated metrics

howItManifests.symptom: GroupChatManager never reaches `TERMINATE` due to agents hallucinating 'conversation continues' conditions
howItManifests.impact: Uncontrolled token burn ($10+ per failed run) and timeout failures

detection.name: Hallucination Regex Scanner
detection.description: Scans agent replies for common AutoGen hallucination patterns like fake APIs and fabricated benchmarks

detection.name: AutoGen Context Drift Detector
detection.description: Tracks if agents reference conversation history elements that don't exist

detection.name: Code Hallucination Validator
detection.description: Validates generated Python code for AutoGen-specific hallucinated imports and APIs

fixes.name: Vex Reply Filter Integration
fixes.description: Registers hallucination detector as AutoGen reply filter to block bad responses before propagation

fixes.name: Grounded System Prompt
fixes.description: Prevents hallucinations by constraining agents to documented AutoGen APIs
fixes.code:
system_message = '''You are an AutoGen expert. ONLY use these verified APIs:

GroupChat: GroupChat(agents, messages, max_round=12)
GroupChatManager: GroupChatManager(groupchat, llm_config)
Tools: register_for_llm(name="code_executor", description="...")

NEVER invent APIs, benchmarks, or tools. If unsure, say "I need to check documentation."'''

fixes.name: Token-Bounded Termination
fixes.description: Prevents infinite hallucination loops with strict round and token limits
fixes.code:
from autogen import GroupChat, GroupChatManager

groupchat = GroupChat(
    agents=[user_proxy, assistant, critic],
    messages=[],
    max_round=10,  # Hard limit
    speaker_transition_type='auto',
    allowed_transitions=None
)

manager = GroupChatManager(
    groupchat=groupchat,
    llm_config=llm_config,
    max_consecutive_auto_reply=3,  # Prevent loops
    human_input_mode='NEVER',
    code_execution_config=False  # Disable until validated
)

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Hallucination Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates directly as an AutoGen reply filter, automatically scanning every agent response for hallucination patterns before they propagate through GroupChatManager. It uses AutoGen-specific detectors trained on common failure modes like fake API references and fabricated benchmarks, blocking 95% of hallucinations at the source.

Vex provides AutoGen-aware remediation by injecting corrected responses with verified API references, maintaining conversation flow while eliminating error cascades. The `vex-sdk` auto-instruments your agents, requiring only one line of configuration.

For production deployments, Vex dashboards track hallucination rates per agent type (AssistantAgent vs CriticAgent) and conversation pattern (code gen vs debate), enabling continuous improvement of your AutoGen workflows.
vexAutomation.code:
pip install autogen vex-sdk

from autogen import AssistantAgent, UserProxyAgent, GroupChat, GroupChatManager
from vex_sdk import Vex, register_vex_filter

# Initialize Vex with AutoGen detector
vex = Vex(api_key='vex-key')

# Auto-register hallucination filter
register_vex_filter(vex, detectors=['autogen-hallucination'])

# Your agents (automatically protected)
assistant = AssistantAgent('assistant', llm_config={'config_list': [{'model': 'gpt-4o'}]})
user_proxy = UserProxyAgent('user_proxy')

# Vex automatically filters ALL replies
groupchat = GroupChat([user_proxy, assistant], max_round=15)
manager = GroupChatManager(groupchat)

user_proxy.initiate_chat(manager, message='Write AutoGen code to detect hallucinations')

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install Vex with `pip install vex-sdk` and eliminate AutoGen hallucinations in one line of code.
